A little FORTRAN program that filters an isf file and extract only those lines 


e.g.
```
isf_file_filter < GERES_2017_09_03.isf
```
should give
```
GERES   4.71 319.8 Pn       2017-09-03T02:56:11.975 137.2 14.0  7.6  613580235  995101753  48.8451   13.7016
GERES   4.71 319.8 Sn       2017-09-03T02:57:04.683 146.7 30.5  1.5  613580235  995101754  48.8451   13.7016
GERES  88.70  40.3 P        2017-09-03T03:34:32.425 235.4  3.5  4.0  613580237  995101785  48.8451   13.7016
GERES  73.69 321.5 P        2017-09-03T03:41:36.625  43.2  6.0 28.7  613580239  995102006  48.8451   13.7016
GERES  73.69 321.5 PKiKP    2017-09-03T03:47:31.611  18.4  3.4  3.6  613580239  995102007  48.8451   13.7016
GERES  76.19 318.0 P        2017-09-03T07:03:58.825 101.1  6.9 10.8  613580258  995102690  48.8451   13.7016
GERES  75.32 343.4 P        2017-09-03T08:33:00.629  13.1  6.0  3.7  613580263  995103038  48.8451   13.7016
GERES  83.51 329.3 P        2017-09-03T09:14:16.000  18.0  6.0  6.1  613580267  995103211  48.8451   13.7016
GERES 144.43 331.7 PKPab    2017-09-03T11:32:34.200  27.7  4.4  5.0  613580274  995103452  48.8451   13.7016
GERES  35.08 314.9 P        2017-09-03T12:55:54.275 105.0  9.8  4.9  613580285  995103741  48.8451   13.7016
GERES 150.13 344.2 PKPbc    2017-09-03T14:02:57.950  17.9  3.5 13.3  613580292  995103935  48.8451   13.7016
GERES 150.13 344.2 PKPab    2017-09-03T14:03:06.225  47.7  2.0  3.8  613580292  995103936  48.8451   13.7016
GERES  76.95  33.7 P        2017-09-03T17:01:12.375   0.0  4.3  3.6  613580312  995104287  48.8451   13.7016
GERES  76.94  33.7 P        2017-09-03T17:53:31.350 340.3  7.5  4.2  613580316  995104439  48.8451   13.7016
GERES 150.19 344.1 PKPbc    2017-09-03T19:59:01.975  28.8  3.0  7.4  613580323  995104700  48.8451   13.7016
GERES  85.68 322.2 P        2017-09-03T22:57:14.025  30.3  6.0  5.0  613580332  995104929  48.8451   13.7016
```

compile by typing
```
make isf_file_filter
```
